Question: Consent to record + use anonymized quotes in investor materials. Name/company omitted by default; title/role OK unless you say otherwise.
What's your role? Team size? Rough % of your work that involves AI tools right now?
Which AI tools are you using weekly? (Need 2+ of: Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Copilot, Perplexity, Figma AI)
Walk me through the last time you had to re-explain context to Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor. What happened, and how did it feel?
Across the AI tools you use, where does context live? When you switch tools, what do you lose?
How long does it typically take to get back into a session from yesterday or last week?
Do Claude Projects, ChatGPT memory, or Cursor's rules already solve this for you?
If someone built a 'project management app for AI work' that you had to open and work inside — would you use it? Why or why not?
How does your team share what people have done with AI? Slack? Shared docs? Not at all?
In the last 30 days, can you name a moment two people on your team solved the same thing with AI separately?
If a teammate disappeared tomorrow, what AI work of theirs would be lost — and would anyone notice?
Does Slack + shared Notion already cover this? Where specifically does it break down?
Does your team have a named process for working WITH AI? What do you call it? (Listen for: 'we don't really,' 'kind of Agile,' 'we're figuring it out.')
Tell me about a time your team's existing process — Agile, Scrum, Shape Up, whatever — felt *wrong* for AI-assisted work.
Does AI ever feel like it's running too fast for your team's rhythm? Or too slow?

If I said 'we run a Fish model — Head phase is explore, Body is build, Tail is ship, and every card is sized bigger-vs-smaller and known-vs-unknown' — does any part of that vocabulary map to something missing in your world?
Is Agile-with-AI basically fine for you, just faster? What would have to change for you to switch frameworks?
Walk me through the last AI tool you installed. What happened?
Any AI tool you wanted to try but gave up on? Why?
When you install something AI-powered on your work machine, what goes through your head? Any hesitation about permissions, data, what it's watching?
For you, is install complexity the blocker, or is it something else — time, priority, permission from IT?
Does anyone on your team use personal Claude or ChatGPT accounts for work? (Ask casually — surfaces shadow AI without judgment.)
If your VP asked 'is AI paying off for your team,' could you answer with data?
Has your company's security or legal team raised AI usage concerns? What happened?
What didn't I ask about that I should have? What AI-workflow pain have you hit that none of my questions touched?
What pain in your AI workflow would you pay — actual money, not 'I'd try it' — to make go away?
Thank the interviewee.
Ask for 1 referral (design leader or hands-on practitioner who should be in this research).
Confirm anonymization preferences — name/company omitted, title/role OK, quotes OK.
